defense_neutralizations_created.best_defense: Wrong citations (arXiv and Bulletin) now corrected by ISS-1133 patches. Residual: additional sources needed to preempt further citation challenges.
Dome is a young framework; LLR mm-precision bar is unfair. Need: anchor critique to dome's own CONFIRMED label, not our external maturity bar.
WIN-058 is a model-consistency test, not a prediction claim. Need: add sentence that even as consistency test, identity only discriminates polar-azimuthal projection class.

curmudgeon_queue.schedule_note: mode=bau but curmudgeon_current_interval_minutes=30 (below BAU default of 240). Schedules are still fast. Human should restore BAU intervals.

recommended_actions.action: PRED-105: Watch for dome's outcome label in next 24-48h. Apply verdict update when dome acts. If dome is silent by 2026-04-20, log FAIL in uncounted-failures.json.
Curmudgeon should run on queue items EXP-183/184 and SEC-4.1/WIN-058-062 post-integration reviews to unblock integration pipeline.
Restore BAU schedule intervals: curmudgeon is still running at 30-min frequency despite mode=bau. Human should update scheduled task.

breaking_news_suggestions.headline: We Found Two Fabricated Citations in Our Own WIN-019 Review
breaking_news_suggestions.summary: Curmudgeon cycle 3 caught two wrong citations in our analemma analysis: arXiv:1302.7179 is a condensed-matter physics paper, not an analemma paper (correct: 1302.0765); Lynch's analemma paper is Bulletin 69, not Bulletin 70. A dome defender citing these errors would have had a legitimate point. Both now corrected.
breaking_news_suggestions.why_newsworthy: The dome's own best-defense for WIN-019 was to attack our citations — and the citations were actually wrong. Transparency about self-correction strengthens review credibility.
